summary: News articles published within the previous 24 hours have outlined a series of recent developments in small UAS operations. Ukraine's drone operators have wiped out a Russian FPV drone crew on the Huliaipole front, while a Ukrainian FPV drone invulnerable to electronic warfare has been used to threaten the Russian border. The Ukrainian CD-T10 radar system has been reported to detect FPV drones and guided aerial bombs within a 15 km radius, and Ukraine has unveiled the Gnome-NS, a new ground "mother drone" for carrying FPVs. Additionally, a T-80BVM tank survived 18 Ukrainian FPV drone strikes, and Ukraine's Ptashka set a new strike quadcopter record by flying on a glass thread undetectable by Russian forces.
